message:
"But my cat never goes outside — does she really need the vet?" 🩺🐱

It's one of the most common things cat parents believe, and it's worth gently setting straight: indoor cats need regular vet care just as much as outdoor cats do. Living inside protects them from a lot of dangers, but it doesn't make them immune to things like dental disease, kidney trouble, thyroid issues, arthritis, or weight gain.

Here's the tricky part: cats are masters at hiding when they feel unwell. By the time something is obvious, it may have been brewing for a while. That's exactly why a yearly checkup matters — and twice a year for seniors (roughly 8+). These visits catch the quiet problems early, when they're easiest to treat.

Think of it as the boring kind of love. 💛 The kind that keeps them purring on your lap for years to come.

When was your cat's last checkup? 🐾
